pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 22 and export_result.sh line 24; consistent across all task scripts
Verified in task.json description and export_result.sh line 80; consistent file naming

Web search confirmed all three are real US government websites: usa.gov is official portal for government services, weather.gov is National Weather Service, nasa.gov is NASA official website. Also verified in setup_task.sh line 39 and export_result.sh line 121

Verified as standard mobile breakpoint (iPhone width) in 2025 responsive design standards from BrowserStack, Framer, DEV Community guides. Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py

Verified as standard tablet breakpoint in Bootstrap and 2025 responsive design guides. Standard for iPad and tablet devices. Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py

Verified as standard desktop breakpoint in 2025 responsive design guides (BrowserStack, DEV Community). Used for large desktop displays with extended content widths. Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py

Verified in export_result.sh lines 44-77 where files are checked with pattern matching for .png, .jpg, .jpeg extensions. Naming convention matches 3 sites × 3 viewports = 9 files. Consistent with task description and file enumeration in verifier.py

privileged_info_summary: The task audits responsive design of three real public US government websites: usa.gov (official government services portal), weather.gov (National Weather Service), and nasa.gov (NASA official site). Screenshots are captured at three standard responsive design breakpoints: 375px (mobile/iPhone), 768px (tablet/iPad), and 1440px (desktop). The breakpoints are industry-standard values confirmed in 2025 responsive design guidelines. Output files follow a consistent naming convention with 9 expected screenshot files (3 sites × 3 viewports), plus a text report. All data references real, publicly accessible websites rather than synthetic test data.
